enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted app for building a hierarchical personal knowledge base.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Trilium Notes: free, open-source, self-hosted app for organizing hierarchical personal notes with encryption and scripting.

features.title: Hierarchical notes & cloning
features.description: Organize notes in a tree structure, with the ability to clone a single note into multiple locations.

features.title: Note encryption
features.description: Password-protect individual notes using AES-128-CBC encryption.

features.title: Multiple note types
features.description: Create text, code, file, canvas, Mermaid diagram, and mindmap notes.

features.title: Built-in collections
features.description: Use calendar, table, Kanban board, geomap, and presentation views for notes.

features.title: Web clipper
features.description: Save web pages into Trilium via a browser extension.

features.title: REST API and scripting
features.description: Automate and extend Trilium using a REST API and custom scripts.

features.title: Note revisions
features.description: Automatic background versioning with undo support.

pros: Fully free and open source with no paid tier
Self-hosted, keeping data under the user's control
Handles very large note collections (100,000+ notes)
Supports encryption for sensitive notes
Highly extensible via scripting and a REST API
cons: Requires self-hosting or running a local server for sync
No official mobile app (only an unofficial community Android client and PWA support)
Steeper learning curve than simple note apps due to its attribute and scripting system

faqs.question: Is Trilium Notes free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Trilium Notes is free and open source with no paid tier.

faqs.question: Can I sync Trilium across devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, by running a self-hosted Trilium server; a PWA is also available for mobile browser access.

faqs.question: Is there an official mobile app?
faqs.answer: Not an official one; an unofficial Android client (TriliumDroid) exists.

faqs.question: How does Trilium store data?
faqs.answer: Notes are stored locally in a SQLite database.

faqs.question: Can I encrypt notes?
faqs.answer: Yes, individual notes can be password-protected with AES-128-CBC encryption.

use_cases.title: Personal knowledge base
use_cases.description: Build a structured, searchable archive of personal notes and research using hierarchical organization and note linking.

use_cases.title: Developer documentation & scripting
use_cases.description: Use the REST API and note scripting to build dynamic, automated note workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Trilium Notes?
paragraphs: Trilium Notes is an open-source note-taking application for building a personal knowledge base, organizing notes in a hierarchical tree with support for cloning notes into multiple locations.
It stores notes locally in a SQLite database and can be run as a desktop application or accessed through a self-hosted server via a browser or progressive web app.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Trilium Notes is for
paragraphs: Trilium Notes suits individuals who want a private, self-hosted place to organize large volumes of hierarchical notes, especially those comfortable with self-hosting a server and using scripting for automation.

enrichment.short_description: Joplin is a free, open-source note-taking app with end-to-end encryption and an optional paid Joplin Cloud sync service.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Joplin: free, open-source, end-to-end encrypted note-taking app for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android and iOS, with optional Joplin Cloud sync.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, and a terminal client.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: Secure notes with E2EE across supported sync targets.

features.title: Multiple sync options
features.description: Sync via Joplin Cloud, Nextcloud, Dropbox, OneDrive, or WebDAV.

features.title: Web clipper
features.description: Save articles and pages using the Chrome or Firefox browser extension.

features.title: Plugin & theme support
features.description: Extend the app with community plugins and custom themes.

features.title: Rich & Markdown editors
features.description: Switch between Rich Text and Markdown editing modes.

features.title: Note publishing
features.description: Publish individual notes to the web via Joplin Cloud.

pros: Core app is free and open source across all major platforms
End-to-end encryption available for privacy
Multiple free sync backend options besides the paid cloud service
Active development with 55.6k GitHub stars and frequent releases
Extensible with community plugins and themes
cons: Hosted collaboration and larger storage require a paid Joplin Cloud subscription
Notebook sharing is receive-only on the cheapest Cloud plan
Self-hosting Joplin Server for teams requires additional technical setup

faqs.question: Is Joplin free?
faqs.answer: The Joplin app is free and open source; Joplin Cloud is a separate paid sync and hosting service.

faqs.question: What does Joplin Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at €2.99/month for the Basic tier, with Pro and Teams tiers also available.

faqs.question: Can I sync without paying?
faqs.answer: Yes, Joplin supports free sync via Nextcloud, Dropbox, OneDrive, or WebDAV.

faqs.question: Is Joplin encrypted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Joplin supports end-to-end encryption (E2EE).

faqs.question: What platforms does Joplin support?
faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, and a terminal app.

use_cases.title: Private note-taking
use_cases.description: Keep encrypted notes synced across devices without relying on a single proprietary cloud provider.

use_cases.title: Team notebook sharing
use_cases.description: Use the Joplin Cloud Teams plan to share notebooks and manage billing centrally.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Joplin?
paragraphs: Joplin is an open-source note-taking application that supports multimedia content, end-to-end encryption, and Markdown editing across desktop, mobile, and terminal clients.

The core app is free; Joplin Cloud is a paid managed sync service (created by developer Laurent Cozic and operated from France) offering hosted storage, sharing, and publishing on top of the free app.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Joplin is for
paragraphs: Joplin fits privacy-focused users who want a free, encrypted, cross-platform note app, and teams that want an affordable managed sync option through Joplin Cloud instead of self-hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Standard Notes is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted note-taking app with free and paid subscription plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Standard Notes: open-source, end-to-end encrypted notes app for Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS and Android, with free and paid plans.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: All notes are encrypted before syncing across devices.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Native apps for Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android.

features.title: Unlimited device sync
features.description: The free tier includes syncing across unlimited devices.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: The web app can be deployed on a user-run server.

features.title: Listed publishing
features.description: Publish notes as an online publication through the Listed feature.

features.title: Third-party extensions
features.description: Extend functionality with community-built extensions.

pros: Open source (AGPL-3.0) with a free tier
Strong focus on end-to-end encryption
Self-hostable for full data control
Cross-platform native apps
cons: Advanced features like extended history, larger file storage, and family sharing require paid Productivity or Professional plans
Exact plan pricing is not consistently published outside the app or website checkout flow

faqs.question: Is Standard Notes free?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a free plan; paid Productivity and Professional plans unlock more storage, note history, and sharing features.

faqs.question: Is Standard Notes open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is licensed under AGPL-3.0 with source code available on GitHub.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Standard Notes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the web app can be deployed behind your own web server.

faqs.question: What platforms are supported?
faqs.answer: Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android.

use_cases.title: Encrypted personal notes
use_cases.description: Store notes with end-to-end encryption synced across all your devices.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployment
use_cases.description: Run the Standard Notes web client on a self-managed server for full control over data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Standard Notes?
paragraphs: Standard Notes is an open-source note-taking application built around end-to-end encryption, letting users capture notes and files in a secure, encrypted store.
Its web client is self-hostable, and it ships native apps for Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android, alongside a free tier and paid Productivity and Professional subscriptions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Standard Notes is for
paragraphs: Standard Notes is aimed at privacy-conscious individuals and organizations that want an encrypted, cross-platform note app with the option to self-host the client.

enrichment.short_description: Notesnook is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted note-taking app with a free plan and paid Essential, Pro, and Believer tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Notesnook: open-source, end-to-end encrypted notes app for web, desktop, iOS and Android, with a free plan and paid Pro tiers.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: Notes are encrypted on-device before syncing, using XChaCha20-Poly1305 and Argon2.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and web.

features.title: Self-hostable sync server
features.description: Run your own Notesnook sync server instead of the hosted cloud.

features.title: Bidirectional note linking
features.description: Link related notes together for easier navigation.

features.title: Password-protected vault
features.description: Store sensitive notes behind an additional password.

features.title: Web clipper
features.description: Save web articles directly into Notesnook.

features.title: Rich editing
features.description: Tables, task lists, formulas, and code blocks in the editor.

pros: Fully open source (GPL-3.0), including the self-hostable sync server
Strong client-side encryption on both free and paid plans
Affordable entry-level paid tier (Essential, from €2.11/month)
Cross-platform coverage across web, desktop, iOS, and Android
cons: Free plan is capped at 50MB/month storage and 50 notebooks/tags
Larger file sizes and unlimited notebooks require a paid Pro or Believer plan

faqs.question: Is Notesnook free?
faqs.answer: Yes, there's a free plan with 50MB/month storage; paid Essential, Pro, and Believer plans add more storage and features.

faqs.question: Is Notesnook open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's fully open source, including the client apps and the self-hostable sync server.

faqs.question: What does the cheapest paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Essential plan starts at €2.11/month, billed yearly.

faqs.question: Does Notesnook offer a refund?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day money-back guarantee applies to paid plans.

faqs.question: What platforms does Notesnook support?
faqs.answer: Web, Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

use_cases.title: Private note-taking
use_cases.description: Encrypt and sync notes across devices without exposing plaintext data to the server.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted sync
use_cases.description: Run the open-source Notesnook sync server to keep encrypted notes on infrastructure you control.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Notesnook?
paragraphs: Notesnook is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted note-taking app positioned as a private alternative to Evernote, encrypting all data on-device before it reaches the server.
It ships native apps for web, Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android, and its sync server is self-hostable, alongside a hosted free plan and paid Essential, Pro, and Believer tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Notesnook is for
paragraphs: Notesnook suits privacy-conscious individuals who want strong client-side encryption across all their devices, and technical users who prefer self-hosting the sync server.

enrichment.short_description: Memos is a free, open-source, self-hosted timeline app for quick Markdown notes and daily logs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Memos: a free, open-source, self-hosted Markdown note-taking app for quick capture, with zero telemetry by default.

features.title: Timeline-first capture
features.description: Write quick notes without folders, templates, or titles.

features.title: Markdown-native storage
features.description: Notes are stored as portable Markdown files.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Runs as one Go binary in an approximately 20MB Docker image.

features.title: Flexible database backends
features.description: Supports SQLite by default, with MySQL or PostgreSQL for larger deployments.

features.title: REST and gRPC APIs
features.description: Build integrations and automations on top of Memos.

features.title: Zero telemetry
features.description: No tracking is enabled by default.

features.title: Tagging and search
features.description: Organize and find notes with tags and full-text search.

pros: Completely free and open source (MIT license) with no paid tier
Lightweight, single-binary deployment via Docker, Raspberry Pi, VPS, or NAS
Zero telemetry by default
Notes stored in portable Markdown format
Actively developed project with 61.6k GitHub stars
cons: Self-hosting is required; there is no official hosted or cloud version
Minimal organizational structure (no folders or notebooks) by design
Users are responsible for their own backups and infrastructure

faqs.question: Is Memos free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memos is completely free and open source with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host Memos?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memos is self-hosted; you deploy it via Docker or as a standalone binary.

faqs.question: What license is Memos under?
faqs.answer: The MIT license.

faqs.question: Does Memos have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers both REST and gRPC APIs.

faqs.question: What databases does Memos support?
faqs.answer: SQLite by default, with MySQL and PostgreSQL available for larger deployments.

use_cases.title: Quick daily logging
use_cases.description: Capture quick notes, links, and snippets in a private timeline without organizational overhead.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted note timeline
use_cases.description: Deploy Memos on a VPS or NAS for a private, ad-free note timeline with no telemetry.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Memos?
paragraphs: Memos is an open-source, self-hosted timeline app for quick notes, daily logs, links, and snippets, built as a single lightweight Go binary.
Notes are stored in portable Markdown, and the project supports SQLite, MySQL, or PostgreSQL, with zero telemetry enabled by default.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Memos is for
paragraphs: Memos fits self-hosters, developers, and privacy-conscious users who want a fast, low-overhead place to jot quick notes without folders or templates.

enrichment.short_description: SilverBullet is a free, open-source, self-hosted, browser-based Markdown note app with built-in scripting and queries.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SilverBullet: a free, open-source, self-hosted, browser-based personal knowledge base with Markdown editing and Lua scripting.

features.title: Live-preview Markdown editor
features.description: Edit notes with wiki-style bidirectional linking.

features.title: Built-in query language (SLIQ)
features.description: Query your notes like a lightweight database.

features.title: Space Lua scripting
features.description: Script dynamic content and automation directly inside notes.

features.title: Single-binary or Docker deployment
features.description: Self-host on a VPS, Raspberry Pi, or via Docker.

features.title: Page templates and custom commands
features.description: Extend the editor with templates and widgets.

features.title: Task management and outlining
features.description: Built-in support for task lists and outlining.

pros: Free and open source (MIT license) with no paid tier
Fully self-hosted, keeping notes under user control
Combines note-taking with a programmable database and query layer
Lightweight deployment as a single binary, Docker container, or CLI tool
cons: Requires self-hosting; there is no official hosted cloud version
Programmable features like Space Lua scripting assume some technical comfort
Browser-based editing depends on running your own server instance

faqs.question: Is SilverBullet free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host SilverBullet?
faqs.answer: Yes, it runs as a self-hosted single binary or Docker container.

faqs.question: What is Space Lua?
faqs.answer: SilverBullet's built-in Lua scripting environment for dynamic content and automation.

faqs.question: What is SLIQ?
faqs.answer: SilverBullet's built-in query language for treating notes as structured data.

use_cases.title: Programmable knowledge base
use_cases.description: Build a personal wiki with queries and scripted automation layered over Markdown notes.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted private notes
use_cases.description: Run a lightweight, browser-based note system on your own server or Raspberry Pi.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SilverBullet?
paragraphs: SilverBullet is an open-source, self-hosted, browser-based personal knowledge base that combines a live-preview Markdown editor with wiki-style linking, a built-in query language, and Lua scripting.
It runs as a single binary, CLI tool, or Docker container with a Rust-based backend and TypeScript frontend, and is free with no paid tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SilverBullet is for
paragraphs: SilverBullet suits technically comfortable users who want a programmable, self-hosted alternative to note apps, treating notes as both documents and queryable data.

enrichment.short_description: Documize is a self-hosted documentation and knowledge management platform with a free tier for up to 5 users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Documize: self-hosted docs and knowledge management platform with content workflows, free for up to 5 users, plans from $900/year.

features.title: Spaces and structured docs
features.description: Organize documentation into spaces with labels.

features.title: Content workflows
features.description: Draft, review, approve, and publish content through defined lifecycle stages.

features.title: Reusable templates and blocks
features.description: Build documents from reusable content blocks and templates.

features.title: Granular permissions
features.description: Control access at the space, document, and action level.

features.title: Built-in analytics
features.description: Track content usage and identify knowledge gaps.

features.title: Multiple auth methods
features.description: Supports LDAP, Active Directory, Keycloak, and CAS authentication.

features.title: Self-hosted single binary
features.description: Deploy on Windows, macOS, Linux, or FreeBSD.

pros: Free tier for up to 5 users with no credit card required
Self-hosted, keeping documentation on the customer's own infrastructure
Open-source Community edition available on GitHub under AGPL-3.0
Supports multiple databases, including PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL, MariaDB, and Percona
Built-in approval workflows and analytics not found in simpler wiki tools
cons: Paid plans are billed annually ($900-$1,800/year) rather than monthly
Requires self-hosting and database setup rather than instant SaaS signup
Smaller open-source community than larger alternatives (2.4k GitHub stars)

faqs.question: Is Documize free?
faqs.answer: Yes, for up to 5 users; paid Essential ($900/year, up to 100 users) and Enterprise ($1,800/year, unlimited users) plans are also available.

faqs.question: Is Documize open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Community edition is available on GitHub under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Is Documize self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it runs as a self-hosted single binary deployed on your own server.

faqs.question: What databases does Documize support?
faqs.answer: PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL, MariaDB, and Percona.

faqs.question: Does Documize offer refunds?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 90-day refund policy is stated for paid plans.

use_cases.title: Internal documentation
use_cases.description: Use the free tier to build internal docs and wikis for up to 5 users.

use_cases.title: Enterprise knowledge management
use_cases.description: Deploy Documize with LDAP/AD authentication, approval workflows, and analytics for larger user bases.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Documize?
paragraphs: Documize is a self-hosted knowledge management platform combining docs, wikis, templates, approval workflows, and analytics into a single deployable binary.
It offers a free tier for up to 5 users, an open-source Community edition on GitHub, and paid Essential and Enterprise plans billed annually for larger organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Documize is for
paragraphs: Documize suits small teams that want free, self-hosted internal documentation, and larger organizations that need enterprise authentication, approval workflows, and content analytics.

enrichment.short_description: HedgeDoc is a free, open-source, self-hosted, real-time collaborative Markdown editor.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HedgeDoc: free, open-source, self-hosted, real-time collaborative Markdown editor with presentation mode and version history.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Multiple users can edit the same Markdown document simultaneously.

features.title: Presentation mode
features.description: Turn Markdown notes into slide decks using reveal.js.

features.title: Diagrams and graphs
features.description: Embed diagrams directly inside documents.

features.title: Permission management
features.description: Control who can view and edit each note.

features.title: Revision history
features.description: Track and restore previous versions of a document.

features.title: Low system requirements
features.description: Runs on low-powered hardware, including a Raspberry Pi.

features.title: Docker deployment
features.description: Ready-to-use Docker images and bundles for self-hosting.

pros: Free and open source (AGPL-3.0) with no paid tier
Real-time multi-user collaborative editing
Lightweight enough to run on a Raspberry Pi
Self-hosted, keeping documents under your own control
Active successor project (HedgeDoc 2) in development
cons: Requires self-hosting; there is no official hosted cloud version
HedgeDoc 1.x is in maintenance mode only, with new features going into the HedgeDoc 2 rewrite
No native mobile or desktop apps; it is browser-based only

faqs.question: Is HedgeDoc free?
faqs.answer: Yes, HedgeDoc is free, open source, and self-hosted.

faqs.question: What license does HedgeDoc use?
faqs.answer: AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Can multiple people edit the same document?
faqs.answer: Yes, HedgeDoc supports real-time collaborative editing.

faqs.question: Was HedgeDoc previously called something else?
faqs.answer: Yes, it was formerly known as CodiMD before rebranding to HedgeDoc in 2020.

faqs.question: Can HedgeDoc create presentations?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a built-in presentation mode powered by reveal.js.

use_cases.title: Collaborative meeting notes
use_cases.description: Co-edit Markdown notes and documents together in real time.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted presentations
use_cases.description: Write Markdown and present it directly using HedgeDoc's built-in presentation mode.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HedgeDoc?
paragraphs: HedgeDoc is an open-source, self-hosted, web-based collaborative Markdown editor that lets multiple users edit the same document in real time.
Formerly known as CodiMD before a 2020 rebrand, it is licensed under AGPL-3.0 and is free to run on your own infrastructure, from a Raspberry Pi to a full server.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HedgeDoc is for
paragraphs: HedgeDoc fits individuals and teams that need real-time collaborative note-taking, presentations, and diagrams while keeping documents on self-hosted infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Mattermost is an open-core, self-hostable team collaboration platform with free self-hosted and paid enterprise editions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mattermost: open-core team messaging and collaboration platform for self-hosted or cloud deployment, with Professional and Enterprise plans.

features.title: Channel-based messaging
features.description: Threaded discussions and channel-based team communication.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Playbooks for automating recurring workflows and incident response.

features.title: Audio calls
features.description: Built-in audio calls with transcription and AI summarization on paid tiers.

features.title: Kanban boards
features.description: Built-in project and task management boards.

features.title: Deployment flexibility
features.description: Self-hosted, private cloud, or major public cloud deployment (AWS, Azure, GCP, Oracle).

features.title: Compliance controls
features.description: FIPS 140-3 compliance and STIG-hardened images on Enterprise tiers.

features.title: Microsoft Teams interoperability
features.description: Shared channels with Microsoft Teams on Enterprise plans.

pros: Open-core project with a self-hosted, open-source base (38.5k GitHub stars)
Deployable on-premises, in private clouds, or in air-gapped environments
Native desktop and mobile apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android
Enterprise tiers add compliance features (FIPS, STIG, legal hold) for regulated industries
cons: Advanced AI, compliance, and scaling features require paid Enterprise or Enterprise Advanced tiers
Pricing for paid plans is not published; requires contacting sales
Enterprise-focused feature set may be more than small teams need

faqs.question: Is Mattermost open source?
faqs.answer: It's an open-core project with source code on GitHub; a compiled edition is released monthly under the MIT license alongside separately licensed enterprise features.

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Mattermost?
faqs.answer: Yes, a self-hosted open-source edition is available; paid Professional, Enterprise, and Enterprise Advanced tiers add features and support.

faqs.question: How much does Mattermost cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing for paid tiers is not published; you need to contact sales for a quote.

faqs.question: Where is Mattermost headquartered?
faqs.answer: Palo Alto, California, USA.

faqs.question: Can Mattermost run air-gapped?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise Advanced tier supports air-gapped and classified deployments.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted team chat
use_cases.description: Deploy the open-source edition on-premises for full control over messaging infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Regulated and government collaboration
use_cases.description: Use Enterprise Advanced for classified, air-gapped, or compliance-heavy deployments.
use_cases.audience: Government, defense, and critical infrastructure organizations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mattermost?
paragraphs: Mattermost is an open-core collaboration platform offering chat, workflow automation, voice calling, and AI-assisted features, deployable self-hosted, in a private cloud, or air-gapped.
Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, Mattermost pairs a free open-source base with paid Professional, Enterprise, and Enterprise Advanced tiers aimed at larger and regulated organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mattermost is for
paragraphs: Mattermost is used by technical teams that want a self-hosted, open-source chat platform, and by government, defense, and critical-infrastructure organizations that need compliance-grade, sovereign deployment options.

enrichment.short_description: Rocket.Chat is an open-source team communications platform with self-hosted, cloud, and custom-quote enterprise plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rocket.Chat: open-source team messaging platform with self-hosted, cloud, and enterprise plans for secure, compliant communication.

features.title: Unified messaging
features.description: Chat, voice calls, video, and screen sharing in one platform.

features.title: Federation
features.description: Federated communication across separate Rocket.Chat organizations.

features.title: Apps-Engine
features.description: Build custom apps and integrations on Rocket.Chat's extensibility framework.

features.title: Deployment flexibility
features.description: Self-hosted (Docker/Kubernetes), cloud, or air-gapped deployment options.

features.title: Compliance certifications
features.description: SOC 2, ISO/IEC 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, and DoD ATO up to IL6.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Manage granular permissions across workspaces.

pros: Open source (MIT license, 45.8k GitHub stars) with a self-hosted free option
Wide deployment flexibility, including air-gapped and classified environments
Broad compliance certification list, including SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA
Founded in 2015 with an established user base across 150+ countries
cons: Commercial, Government, and Defense plan pricing is not published; requires contacting sales
Enterprise compliance focus may add complexity for teams that just need basic team chat
Federation and advanced Apps-Engine features may require technical setup

faqs.question: Is Rocket.Chat open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Rocket.Chat?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports self-hosted deployment via Docker or Kubernetes, including air-gapped environments.

faqs.question: How much does Rocket.Chat cost?
faqs.answer: Paid Commercial, Government, and Defense plans use custom quotes; you need to contact sales for pricing.

faqs.question: When was Rocket.Chat founded?
faqs.answer: It started as a side project of four friends in 2015.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Rocket.Chat have?
faqs.answer: SOC 2, ISO/IEC 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, and DoD Authorization to Operate up to IL6.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted secure team chat
use_cases.description: Deploy Rocket.Chat on your own infrastructure for full data control.

use_cases.title: Government and defense communications
use_cases.description: Use Rocket.Chat's Government and Defense plans for classified or sovereign communication needs.

features: Sovereign communications for public sector agencies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Rocket.Chat?
paragraphs: Rocket.Chat is an open-source communications platform combining messaging, voice, video, and AI-powered automation, founded in 2015 as a side project of four friends.
It supports self-hosted, cloud, and air-gapped deployments, and holds certifications including SOC 2, ISO/IEC 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, and DoD Authorization to Operate up to IL6.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Rocket.Chat is for
paragraphs: Rocket.Chat is used by security-conscious organizations that want a self-hosted, open-source chat platform, and by government and defense agencies that need sovereign or air-gapped communication.
